items.verseEn: And [by] the morning when it brightens,
By the night when it covers

items.verse: فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلْمِحْرَابِ فَأَوْحَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِمْ أَن سَبِّحُوا۟ بُكْرَةًۭ وَعَشِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: So he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt [Allāh] in the morning and afternoon.
And mention the name of your Lord [in prayer] morning and evening

items.verse: وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: And We shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails, [saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you." And they wronged Us not - but they were [only] wronging themselves.

items.verse: وَقَطَّعْنَـٰهُمُ ٱثْنَتَىْ عَشْرَةَ أَسْبَاطًا أُمَمًۭا ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱسْتَسْقَىٰهُ قَوْمُهُۥٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ ۖ فَٱنۢبَجَسَتْ مِنْهُ ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًۭا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍۢ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۚ وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْغَمَـٰمَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: And We divided them into twelve descendant tribes [as distinct] nations. And We inspired to Moses when his people implored him for water, "Strike with your staff the stone," and there gushed forth from it twelve springs. Every people [i.e., tribe] knew its watering place. And We shaded them with clouds and sent down upon them manna and quails, [saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you." And they wronged Us not, but they were [only] wronging themselves.

items.verse: وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًۭا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةًۭ ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And to the Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. There has come to you clear evidence from your Lord. This is the she-camel of Allāh [sent] to you as a sign. So leave her to eat within Allāh's land and do not touch her with harm, lest there seize you a painful punishment.
And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ خُذُوا۟ حِذْرَكُمْ فَٱنفِرُوا۟ ثُبَاتٍ أَوِ ٱنفِرُوا۟ جَمِيعًۭا
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, take your precaution and [either] go forth in companies or go forth all together.
[To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?

items.verse: فَتَقَطَّعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ زُبُرًۭا ۖ كُلُّ حِزْبٍۭ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ
items.verseEn: But they [i.e., the people] divided their religion among them into portions [i.e., sects] - each faction, in what it has, rejoicing.

items.verse: قُلْ هُوَ ٱلْقَادِرُ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَبْعَثَ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابًۭا مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ أَوْ مِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ شِيَعًۭا وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُم بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ۗ ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَفْقَهُونَ
items.verseEn: Say, "He is the [one] Able to send upon you affliction from above you or from beneath your feet or to confuse you [so you become] sects and make you taste the violence of one another." Look how We diversify the signs that they might understand.

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسَ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: Who will inherit al-Firdaus. They will abide therein eternally.

items.verse: وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيهَا كَأْسًۭا كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا زَنجَبِيلًا
items.verseEn: And they will be given to drink a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of ginger
As if they were rubies and coral.

harf.arabicVerse: وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ ۘ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُۥ ۚ لَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
harf.verse: 28:88 — And do not invoke with Allāh another deity. There is no deity except Him. Everything will be destroyed except His Face. His is the judgement, and to Him you will be returned.

deduction.title: The Scorners of the Wind's Warning
deduction.intro: A nation arose from the earth, blessed with immense strength and unmatched skill in construction. Their pride swelled with their accomplishments, causing them to dismiss the warnings of a messenger sent to guide them. Their story is a powerful reminder that no might on Earth can stand against the will of the Divine.
clues: We were made successors on the earth after a great flood had cleansed it.
We were known for our towering structures, the likes of which had not been created in the lands before.
A prophet from among our own people was sent to us, but we accused him of being a liar.
We boasted of our physical power, asking aloud, 'Who is mightier than us in strength?'
We arrogantly challenged our messenger to bring upon us the divine punishment he warned of.
Our end came as a furious, roaring wind that raged for seven nights and eight consecutive days, leaving us like hollow palm trunks.

trial.label: What Was Our Primary Sin?
options: Building a tower to reach the heavens
Plotting to kill a prophet in a well

outcome.label: What Was Our Punishment?
options: A shower of stones of baked clay

deduction.arabic: فَأَمَّا عَادٌۭ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَقَالُوا۟ مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً ۖ أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةًۭ ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ
deduction.verse: As for ʿAad, they were arrogant upon the earth without right and said, "Who is greater than us in strength?" Did they not consider that Allāh who created them was greater than them in strength? But they were rejecting Our signs. (41:15)

scramble.hint: This verse asks a rhetorical question about the ultimate reward for righteous actions.
scramble.verseEn: Is the reward for good [anything] but good?

verse.translation: [Moses] said, "My Lord, expand for me my breast [with assurance]"

theme_question.correct: Seeking Divine assistance and confidence before a monumental task
options: Seeking Divine assistance and confidence before a monumental task
A prayer for material wealth and prosperity
A request for forgiveness for past sins
An expression of gratitude after a victory

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is the opening line of a powerful supplication made by Prophet Musa (Moses) right after Allah commanded him to go to the tyrannical Pharaoh. Recognizing the immense difficulty of the mission, Musa immediately turned to Allah, asking for the inner strength, clarity, and support needed to succeed.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The core theme is absolute reliance on Allah when facing daunting challenges. Musa's prayer teaches us that the first step in any difficult endeavor is to seek divine aid. 'Expanding the breast' is a metaphor for granting courage, confidence, and reassurance. This du'a is a timeless model for anyone feeling overwhelmed, whether by a public speaking engagement, a difficult conversation, or a major life project.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Ta-Ha, named after the two letters that begin it, is a Makkan surah. It was revealed to comfort and strengthen the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) during a period of difficulty. The surah's central narrative is the detailed story of Prophet Musa, from his miraculous infancy to his confrontation with Pharaoh and the journey of the Israelites. It powerfully illustrates Allah's mercy, His control over all affairs, and the ultimate victory of faith over arrogance and disbelief.
